# DSX Exchange

> NVIDIA DSX Exchange — MQTT event bus for AI Factory operations

DSX Exchange is the integration layer within DSX OS that connects AI factory subsystems (GPU clusters, BMS, power distribution, cooling, grid interfaces) through a NATS-based MQTT event bus with AsyncAPI schema contracts.

## Components

- `schema/`: AsyncAPI contracts for MQTT topics and payloads (BMS, power management, NICo, SPIFFE, etc.)
- `auth-callout/`: Go service for NATS auth callout — OAuth2/JWKS, mTLS, NKey, and no-auth profiles
- `deploy/`: Umbrella Helm chart (`nats-event-bus`) bundling NATS, NACK, auth-callout, and Surveyor as subcharts
- `local/`: Kind-based evaluation environment, MQTT functional/performance tests, benchmark tooling
- `fern/`: Fern docs configuration — renders `docs/` as the hosted documentation site
- `docs/`: Canonical documentation (architecture, pre-deployment, deployment, authentication, operations, testing, BMS integration, integrator quickstart)

## Key Concepts

- Each Kubernetes cluster (1 CSC, N CPCs) runs its own NATS event bus instance
- CPC instances federate to CSC via NATS leaf nodes through Kubernetes Gateway API
- MQTT 3.1.1 is the client protocol — a deliberate choice for BMS and OT device compatibility; NATS implements MQTT 3.1.1 only
- MQTT topic separators (`/`) map to NATS subject tokens (`.`) transparently
- QoS 0, 1, and 2 are supported (QoS 1 and 2 backed by JetStream)
- Auth-callout handles all authentication; permissions are topic-level ACLs per client
- The Helm chart is provider-agnostic for secrets — any pipeline that materializes Kubernetes Secrets works (OpenBao, Vault + VSO, sealed-secrets, external-secrets)
- Auth-callout container image must be built from source (not published to a public registry)

## Documentation

- docs/architecture.md — topology, topic spaces, cross-layer routing, MQTT support, federation, component overview
- docs/pre-deployment.md — host prerequisites, infrastructure prerequisites, auth-callout image build, required secrets, NKey generation, secrets pipeline, certificate management, Gateway setup
- docs/getting-started.md — evaluation install, production deployment steps, cross-layer routing, mTLS hostname agreement
- docs/authentication.md — auth model (OAuth2, mTLS, NKey, no-auth), auth mode identifiers, permissions configuration, secrets management
- docs/operations.md — monitoring, metrics reference, configuration tuning, teardown, chart dependencies
- docs/testing.md — functional and performance test coverage, benchmark tooling
- docs/integrator-quickstart.md — MQTT integration guide for application developers
- docs/bms-integration.md — BMS-specific integration patterns

## Tech Stack

- Go 1.25+ (auth-callout, mqtt-client, mqttbs benchmark tool)
- Helm 3.x or 4.x (Helm 4 required only for local evaluation; Helm 3 works for production)
- Kubernetes 1.27+ with Gateway API (Envoy Gateway 1.5+)
- NATS with MQTT 3.1.1, JetStream, leaf-node federation
- AsyncAPI 3.0 schemas
- Fern docs platform
